The power of greeting for the gatekeeper. Look I've made literally tens of thousands of cold calls in my career both door-to-door and over the phone. I've probably made more over the phone than door-to-door just because you can, right? I can pick up the phone and call a lot more people than I can travel to a city, get in the car, drive over someplace and knock on the door. I've developed what I know to be a greeting that communicates to the gatekeeper that you are important and valuable and are elevated and gives you a better chance of getting in to see that person. It sounds like this, hey Crystal this Grant Cardone is John available. It's Grant Cardone is John available. Notice I know the gatekeeper's name, okay? Announce yourself not where you're calling from or who you're with. I didn't say hey I'm with Cardone Enterprises down in Miami. I didn't do any of that. My name's Grant. I would use first name only. I would drop the last name, okay? I would use the prospects first name and I would use the gatekeeper's first name, okay? Crystal, my name's Grant. It's Grant Cardone. If you want to add a second name do it on the pass-through. My name's Grant. It's Grant Cardone. I'm calling for Dick, okay? And use the first name as you know this person. Example of the wrong way to make this call would be hello my name's Grant Cardone. I'm from XYZ company as Mr. Smith in. Never ever ever ever in your life going to work, okay? If it does work you're talking to some cat in an office that has no money. Look I can almost guarantee I mean you heard it that if your call sounds like this you're gonna get further screened. You're probably just gonna get dumped because you sound and have identified yourself as a salesperson. Now no problem being a salesperson but it is a problem when you're just a salesperson for the gatekeeper because remember what their job is not to waste Dick's time. Perfect. To perfect your opening, okay? You got to get your segue prepared. You got to get your segue, okay? My name is and this is why I'm calling. Now you got to have that clear.
